revision.comment: 滋賀県の神社
revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=滋賀縣護國神社| 所在地=滋賀県彦根市尾末町1-59| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|16|30|N|136|15|23|E|}}| 祭神=護国の[[英霊]]| 創建=明治9年（1876年）| 社格=内務大臣指定護国神社・別表神社| 例祭=[[4月5日]]
[[10月5日]]}} '''滋賀縣護國神社'''（しがけんごこくじんじゃ）は、[[滋賀県]][[彦根市]]の[[彦根城]]址にある[[神社]]（[[護国神社]]）である。 [[戊辰戦争]]から[[第二次世界大戦]]までの滋賀県関係の戦歿者3万4千余柱を祀る。 == 歴史 == [[明治]]2年（[1869年]]）、彦根の大洞竜潭寺に戊辰戦争で戦死した彦根藩士26人の霊を祀る招魂碑が建てられた。[[明治]]8年（[[1875年]]）、元[[彦根藩]]主[[井伊直憲]]の主唱により招魂碑を神社に改造する旨の政府の通達が出され、招魂碑を現在地に移し、翌明治9年、同地に社殿を造営・鎮座した。 昭和14年に内務大臣指定護国神社として「滋賀県護國神社」に改称した。第二次大戦後の占領期は「沙々那美神社」と改称していたが、日本が主権を回復した昭和28年に元の社名に復した。 [[Category:滋賀県の神社|しかけんここくしんしや]] [[Category:護国神社|しかけんここくしんしや]] [[Category:別表神社|しかけんここくしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=滋賀縣護國神社| 所在地=滋賀県彦根市尾末町1-59| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|16|30|N|136|15|23|E|}}| 祭神=護国の[[英霊]]| 創建=明治9年（1876年）| 社格=内務大臣指定護国神社・別表神社| 例祭=[[4月5日]]

revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=滋賀縣護國神社| 画像=[[画像:Shigaken-gokoku-jinja02b3200.jpg|300px]]| 所在地=滋賀県彦根市尾末町1-59| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|16|30|N|136|15|23|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| 祭神=護国の[[英霊]]| 創建=明治9年（1876年）| 社格=内務大臣指定護国神社・別表神社| 例祭=[[4月5日]]
[[10月5日]]}} [[画像:Shigaken-gokoku-jinja03b3840.jpg|thumb|220px|拝殿]] '''滋賀縣護國神社'''（しがけんごこくじんじゃ）は、[[滋賀県]][[彦根市]]の[[彦根城]]址にある[[神社]]（[[護国神社]]）である。 [[戊辰戦争]]から[[第二次世界大戦]]までの滋賀県関係の戦歿者3万4千余柱を祀る。 == 歴史 == [[明治]]2年（[[1869年]]）、彦根の大洞竜潭寺に戊辰戦争で戦死した彦根藩士26人の霊を祀る招魂碑が建てられた。[[明治]]8年（[[1875年]]）、元[[彦根藩]]主[[井伊直憲]]の主唱により招魂碑を神社に改造する旨の政府の通達が出され、招魂碑を現在地に移し、翌明治9年、同地に社殿を造営・鎮座した。 昭和14年に内務大臣指定護国神社として「滋賀県護國神社」に改称した。第二次大戦後の占領期は「沙々那美神社」と改称していたが、日本が主権を回復した昭和28年に元の社名に復した。 == 外部リンク == * [http://www.shigagokoku.jp/　滋賀縣護國神社] {{Commonscat|Shigaken-Gokoku-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しかけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:護国神社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:彦根市の歴史]] [[Category:彦根市の建築物]]

{{出典の明記|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:04 (UTC)}} {{神社| 名称=滋賀縣護國神社| 画像=[[画像:Shigaken-gokoku-jinja02b3200.jpg|300px]]| 所在地=滋賀県彦根市尾末町1-59| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|16|30|N|136|15|23|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| 祭神=護国の[[英霊]]| 創建=明治9年（1876年）| 社格=内務大臣指定護国神社・別表神社| 例祭=[[4月5日]]

{{出典の明記|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:04 (UTC)}} {{神社 | 名称=滋賀縣護國神社 | 画像=[[画像:Shigaken-gokoku-jinja02b3200.jpg|300px]] | 所在地=[[滋賀県]][[彦根市]]尾末町1-59 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|16|30|N|136|15|23|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=国事殉難者 | 創建=[[1876年]]（[[明治]]9年） | 社格=[[内務大臣 (日本)|内務大臣]]指定[[護国神社]]・[[別表神社]] | 例祭=[[4月5日]]
[[10月5日]] }} [[画像:Shigaken-gokoku-jinja03b3840.jpg|thumb|220px|拝殿]] '''滋賀縣護國神社'''（しがけんごこくじんじゃ）は、[[滋賀県]][[彦根市]]にある[[神社]]（[[護国神社]]）。[[戊辰戦争]]から[[第二次世界大戦]]までの滋賀県関係の戦歿者3万4千余柱を祀る。現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。 == 歴史 == [[1869年]]（[[明治]]2年）、彦根の大洞[[龍潭寺 (彦根市)|龍潭寺]]に戊辰戦争で戦死した[[彦根藩]]士26人の霊を祀る招魂碑が建てられたのが始まりである。[[1875年]]（明治8年）、元彦根藩主[[井伊直憲]]の主唱により招魂碑を神社に改造する旨の政府の通達が出され、招魂碑を現在地に移し、翌[[1876年]]（明治9年）、同地に社殿を造営・鎮座した。 [[1939年]]（[[昭和]]14年）に[[内務大臣 (日本)|内務大臣]]指定護国神社として「滋賀県護國神社」に改称した。[[太平洋戦争]]後の占領期に「沙々那美神社」と改称し、[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]に加列されている。 日本が主権を回復した[[1953年]]（昭和28年）10月に元の社名に復した。 == 境内 == * 本殿 * 祝詞舎 * 拝殿 * 翼廊 * 滋賀県英霊顕彰館 * 社務所 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Shigaken-Gokoku-jinja}} * [http://www.shigagokoku.jp/　滋賀縣護國神社] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しかけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:護国神社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:彦根市の歴史]] [[Category:彦根市の建築物]]

{{出典の明記|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:04 (UTC)}} {{神社 | 名称=滋賀縣護國神社 | 画像=[[画像:Shigaken-gokoku-jinja02b3200.jpg|300px]] | 所在地=[[滋賀県]][[彦根市]]尾末町1-59 | 位置={{Coord|35|16|30|N|136|15|23|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=国事殉難者 | 創建=[[1876年]]（[[明治]]9年） | 社格=[[内務大臣 (日本)|内務大臣]]指定[[護国神社]]・[[別表神社]] | 例祭=[[4月5日]]
[[10月5日]] | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga Prefecture|label=滋賀縣護國神社|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} [[画像:Shigaken-gokoku-jinja03b3840.jpg|thumb|220px|拝殿]] '''滋賀縣護國神社'''（しがけんごこくじんじゃ）は、[[滋賀県]][[彦根市]]にある[[神社]]（[[護国神社]]）。[[戊辰戦争]]から[[第二次世界大戦]]までの滋賀県関係の戦歿者3万4千余柱を祀る。現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。 == 歴史 == [[1869年]]（[[明治]]2年）、彦根の大洞[[龍潭寺 (彦根市)|龍潭寺]]に戊辰戦争で戦死した[[彦根藩]]士26人の霊を祀る招魂碑が建てられたのが始まりである。[[1875年]]（明治8年）、元彦根藩主[[井伊直憲]]の主唱により招魂碑を神社に改造する旨の政府の通達が出され、招魂碑を現在地に移し、翌[[1876年]]（明治9年）、同地に社殿を造営・鎮座した。 [[1939年]]（[[昭和]]14年）に[[内務大臣 (日本)|内務大臣]]指定護国神社として「滋賀県護國神社」に改称した。[[太平洋戦争]]後の占領期に「沙々那美神社」と改称し、[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]に加列されている。 日本が主権を回復した[[1953年]]（昭和28年）10月に元の社名に復した。 == 境内 == * 本殿 * 祝詞舎 * 拝殿 * 翼廊 * 滋賀県英霊顕彰館 * 社務所 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Shigaken-Gokoku-jinja}} * [http://www.shigagokoku.jp/　滋賀縣護國神社] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しかけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:護国神社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:彦根市の歴史]] [[Category:彦根市の建築物]]

revision.comment: Created by translating the page "[[:ja:Special:Redirect/revision/93895928|滋賀縣護國神社]]"
revision.text:
{{神社|名称=滋賀縣護國神社|画像=[[画像:Shigaken-gokoku-jinja02b3200.jpg|300px]]|所在地=[[滋賀県]][[彦根市]]尾末町1-59|位置={{Coord|35|16|30|N|136|15|23|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|祭神=国事殉難者|創建=[[1876年]]（[[明治]]9年）|社格=[[内務大臣 (日本)|内務大臣]]指定[[護国神社]]・[[別表神社]]|札所等=[[井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり]]第7番|例祭=[[4月5日]]
[[10月5日]]|地図={{Location map|Japan Shiga Prefecture|label=滋賀縣護國神社|float=center|relief=1|width=256}}}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Shigaken-gokoku-jinja02b3200.jpg|map_type=Japan|coordinates=|map_size=|map_alt=|map_relief=|map_caption=|location=|festival=|consecration_year=1869|type=''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'')|leadership=|website=|architecture_style=|founded_by=|established=|date_destroyed=}} {{ill|Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|ja|滋賀県護國神社|sv|Shigaken-gokoku-jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Hikone City, [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]] or a shrine for people who died in wars.{{Cite web|date=2017-09-05|title=日本彦根旅游指南|url=https://visit.hikoneshi.com/en/plan_your_visit/sightseeing/temples-shrines/shiga-gokoku-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-22|language=en-US}} There are more than 34,000 people who died in wars from the prefecture. These people are honored in the shrine. The wars are the [[Boshin War]], the [[Satsuma Rebellion]], the [[first Sino-Japanese War]], the [[Russo-Japanese War]], and [[World War II]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]] which means a highly ranked shrine.{{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} Gokoku shrines were made to honor people who died in wars. They are led by [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They used to be called [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] but the name was changed in 1939.{{Cite journal|last=TAKAYAMA|first=K. PETER|date=1990|title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081|journal=Journal of Church and State|volume=32|issue=3|pages=527–547|issn=0021-969X}} == History == In the year 1869 a memorial was made in {{ill|Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)|lt=Ryutani Temple|ja|龍潭寺_(彦根市)}} for dead soldiers. 26 people died in the [[Boshin War]] from the city and they were honored in the temple. In [[1875]] (Meiji 8), the government said it should be made into a shrine. {{Ill|Ii Naonori|en}}, the lord of Hikone moved it to the current place. In [[1939]], it was renamed 'Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine' by [[Home Ministry|the Minister of Home Affairs]] . After [[World War II]] it was renamed to Sasanami Shrine. It was listed as a [[Beppyo shrine]]. In October [[1953|1953,]] after the occupation ended, the name was changed back. == See Also == * [[Hero shrine]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) == References == {{reflist}} * ; == External link == * [http://www.shigagokoku.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Coordinates on Wikidata]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Shigaken-gokoku-jinja02b3200.jpg|map_type=Japan|coordinates=|map_size=|map_alt=|map_relief=|map_caption=|location=|festival=|consecration_year=1869|type=''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'')|leadership=|website=|architecture_style=|founded_by=|established=|date_destroyed=}} {{ill|Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|ja|滋賀県護國神社|sv|Shigaken-gokoku-jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Hikone City, [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]] or a shrine for people who died in wars.{{Cite web|date=2017-09-05|title=日本彦根旅游指南|url=https://visit.hikoneshi.com/en/plan_your_visit/sightseeing/temples-shrines/shiga-gokoku-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-22|language=en-US}} There are more than 34,000 people who died in wars from the prefecture. These people are honored in the shrine. The wars are the [[Boshin War]], the [[Satsuma Rebellion]], the [[first Sino-Japanese War]], the [[Russo-Japanese War]], and [[World War II]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]] which means a highly ranked shrine.{{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} Gokoku shrines were made to honor people who died in wars. They are led by [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They used to be called [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] but the name was changed in 1939.{{Cite journal|last=TAKAYAMA|first=K. PETER|date=1990|title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081|journal=Journal of Church and State|volume=32|issue=3|pages=527–547|issn=0021-969X}} == History == In the year 1869 a memorial was made in {{ill|Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)|lt=Ryutani Temple|ja|龍潭寺_(彦根市)}} for dead soldiers. 26 people died in the [[Boshin War]] from the city and they were honored in the temple. In [[1875]] (Meiji 8), the government said it should be made into a shrine. {{Ill|Ii Naonori|en}}, the lord of Hikone moved it to the current place. In [[1939]], it was renamed 'Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine' by [[Home Ministry|the Minister of Home Affairs]] . After [[World War II]] it was renamed to Sasanami Shrine. It was listed as a [[Beppyo shrine]]. In October [[1953|1953,]] after the occupation ended, the name was changed back. == See Also == * [[Hero shrine]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) == References == {{reflist}} * ; == External link == * [http://www.shigagokoku.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Coordinates on Wikidata]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Shigaken-gokoku-jinja02b3200.jpg|map_type=Japan|coordinates=|map_size=|map_alt=|map_relief=|map_caption=|location=|festival=|consecration_year=1869|type=''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'')|leadership=|website=|architecture_style=|founded_by=|established=|date_destroyed=}} {{ill|Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|ja|滋賀県護國神社|sv|Shigaken-gokoku-jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Hikone City, [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]] or a shrine for people who died in wars.{{Cite web|date=2017-09-05|title=日本彦根旅游指南|url=https://visit.hikoneshi.com/en/plan_your_visit/sightseeing/temples-shrines/shiga-gokoku-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-22|language=en-US}} There are more than 34,000 people who died in wars from the prefecture. These people are honored in the shrine. The wars are the [[Boshin War]], the [[Satsuma Rebellion]], the [[first Sino-Japanese War]], the [[Russo-Japanese War]], and [[World War II]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]] which means a highly ranked shrine.{{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} Gokoku shrines were made to honor people who died in wars. They are led by [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They used to be called [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] but the name was changed in 1939.{{Cite journal|last=TAKAYAMA|first=K. PETER|date=1990|title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081|journal=Journal of Church and State|volume=32|issue=3|pages=527–547|issn=0021-969X}} == History == In the year 1869 a memorial was made in {{ill|Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)|lt=Ryutani Temple|ja|龍潭寺_(彦根市)}} for dead soldiers. 26 people died in the [[Boshin War]] from the city and they were honored in the temple. In [[1875]] (Meiji 8), the government said it should be made into a shrine. {{Ill|Ii Naonori|en}}, the lord of Hikone moved it to the current place. In [[1939]], it was renamed 'Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine' by [[Home Ministry|the Minister of Home Affairs]] . After [[World War II]] it was renamed to Sasanami Shrine. It was listed as a [[Beppyo shrine]]. In October [[1953|1953,]] after the occupation ended, the name was changed back. == See Also == * [[Hero shrine]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) == References == {{reflist}} * ; == External link == * [http://www.shigagokoku.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Coordinates on Wikidata]] [[Category:Shiga Prefecture]]

These people are honored in the shrine. The wars are the [[Boshin War]], the [[Satsuma Rebellion]], the [[first Sino-Japanese War]], the [[Russo-Japanese War]], and [[World War II]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]] which means a highly ranked shrine. Gokoku shrines were made to honor people who died in wars. They are led by [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They used to be called [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] but the name was changed in 1939. == History == In the year 1869 a memorial was made in {{ill|Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)|lt=Ryutani Temple|ja|龍潭寺_(彦根市)}} for dead soldiers. 26 people died in the [[Boshin War]] from the city and they were honored in the temple. In [[1875]] (Meiji 8), the government said it should be made into a shrine. {{Ill|Ii Naonori|en}}, the lord of Hikone moved it to the current place. In [[1939]], it was renamed 'Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine' by [[Home Ministry|the Minister of Home Affairs]] . After [[World War II]] it was renamed to Sasanami Shrine. It was listed as a [[Beppyo shrine]]. In October [[1953|1953,]] after the occupation ended, the name was changed back. == See Also == * [[Hero shrine]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) == References == * ; == External link == * [http://www.shigagokoku.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Coordinates on Wikidata]] [[Category:Shiga Prefecture]]

{{出典の明記|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:04 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称 = 滋賀縣護國神社 |画像 = [[画像:Shigaken-gokoku-jinja02b3200.jpg|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[滋賀県]][[彦根市]]尾末町1-59 |位置 = {{Coord|35|16|30|N|136|15|23|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 国事殉難者 |創建 = [[1876年]]（[[明治]]9年） |社格 = [[内務大臣 (日本)|内務大臣]]指定[[護国神社]]・[[別表神社]] |札所等 = [[井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり]]第7番 |例祭 = [[4月5日]]
[[10月5日]] |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=滋賀縣護國神社|position=bottom|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|16|30|N|136|15|23|E}}}} }} '''滋賀縣護國神社'''（しがけんごこくじんじゃ）は、[[滋賀県]][[彦根市]]にある[[神社]]（[[護国神社]]）。[[戊辰戦争]]から[[第二次世界大戦]]までの滋賀県関係の戦歿者3万4千余柱を祀る。現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。 == 歴史 == [[1869年]]（[[明治]]2年）、彦根の大洞[[龍潭寺 (彦根市)|龍潭寺]]に[[戊辰戦争]]で戦死した[[彦根藩]]士26人の霊を祀る招魂碑が建てられたのが始まりである。[[1875年]]（明治8年）、元彦根藩主[[井伊直憲]]の主唱により招魂碑を神社に改造する旨の政府の通達が出され、招魂碑を現在地に移し、翌[[1876年]]（明治9年）、同地に社殿を造営・鎮座した。 [[1939年]]（[[昭和]]14年）に[[内務大臣 (日本)|内務大臣]]指定護国神社として「滋賀県護國神社」に改称した。[[太平洋戦争]]後の占領期に「沙々那美神社」と改称し、[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]に加列されている。 日本が主権を回復した[[1953年]]（昭和28年）10月に元の社名に復した。 == 境内 == * 本殿 * 祝詞舎 * 拝殿 * 翼廊 * 滋賀県英霊顕彰館 * 社務所 * 父の像 * 母の像 Shigaken-gokoku-jinja03b3840.jpg|拝殿 == 前後の札所 == ; [[井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり]] : 6 [[埋木舎]]　-　'''7 滋賀縣護國神社'''　-　8 [[長寿院]]（大洞弁財天） == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Shigaken-Gokoku-jinja}} * [http://www.shigagokoku.jp/　滋賀縣護國神社] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しかけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:護国神社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:彦根市の歴史]] [[Category:彦根市の建築物]]

(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'')|leadership=|website=|architecture_style=|founded_by=|established=|date_destroyed=}} {{ill|Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|ja|滋賀県護國神社|sv|Shigaken-gokoku-jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Hikone City, [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]] or a shrine for people who died in wars.{{Cite web|date=2017-09-05|title=日本彦根旅游指南|url=https://visit.hikoneshi.com/en/plan_your_visit/sightseeing/temples-shrines/shiga-gokoku-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-22|language=en-US}} There are more than 34,000 people who died in wars from the prefecture. These people are honored in the shrine. The wars are the [[Boshin War]], the [[Satsuma Rebellion]], the [[first Sino-Japanese War]], the [[Russo-Japanese War]], and [[World War II]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]] which means a highly ranked shrine. Gokoku shrines were made to honor people who died in wars. They are led by [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They used to be called [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] but the name was changed in 1939. == History == In the year 1869 a memorial was made in {{ill|Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)|lt=Ryutani Temple|ja|龍潭寺_(彦根市)}} for dead soldiers. 26 people died in the [[Boshin War]] from the city and they were honored in the temple. In [[1875]] (Meiji 8), the government said it should be made into a shrine. {{Ill|Ii Naonori|en}}, the lord of Hikone moved it to the current place. In [[1939]], it was renamed 'Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine' by [[Home Ministry|the Minister of Home Affairs]] . After [[World War II]] it was renamed to Sasanami Shrine. It was listed as a [[Beppyo shrine]]. (Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'')|leadership=|website=|architecture_style=|founded_by=|established=|date_destroyed=}} {{ill|Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|ja|滋賀県護國神社|sv|Shigaken-gokoku-jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Hikone City, [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]] or a shrine for people who died in wars.{{Cite web|date=2017-09-05|title=日本彦根旅游指南|url=https://visit.hikoneshi.com/en/plan_your_visit/sightseeing/temples-shrines/shiga-gokoku-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-22|language=en-US}} There are more than 34,000 people who died in wars from the prefecture. These people are honored in the shrine. The wars are the [[Boshin War]], the [[Satsuma Rebellion]], the [[first Sino-Japanese War]], the [[Russo-Japanese War]], and [[World War II]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]] which means a highly ranked shrine. Gokoku shrines were made to honor people who died in wars. They are led by [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They used to be called [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] but the name was changed in 1939. == History == In the year 1869 a memorial was made in {{ill|Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)|lt=Ryutani Temple|ja|龍潭寺_(彦根市)}} for dead soldiers. 26 people died in the [[Boshin War]] from the city and they were honored in the temple. In [[1875]] (Meiji 8), the government said it should be made into a shrine. {{Ill|Ii Naonori|en}}, the lord of Hikone moved it to the current place. In [[1939]], it was renamed 'Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine' by [[Home Ministry|the Minister of Home Affairs]] . After [[World War II]] it was renamed to Sasanami Shrine. It was listed as a [[Beppyo shrine]]. In October [[1953]], after the occupation ended, the name was changed back. == Related pages == * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) == References == {{reflist}} == Other websites == * [http://www.shigagokoku.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Shiga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] {{Gokoku Shrines}} [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Coordinates on Wikidata]] [[Category:Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Shiga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Shiga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Shiga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq\]] [[Category:qqq]][[en:Shiga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Shiga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:滋賀縣護國神社]][[sv:Shigaken-gokoku-jinja]][[simple:Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:Shiga Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:Shiga Gokoku Shrine]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]]

In October [[1953]], after the occupation ended, the name was changed back. == Related pages == * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) == References == {{reflist}} == Other websites == * [http://www.shigagokoku.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Shiga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] {{Gokoku Shrines}} [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Coordinates on Wikidata]] [[Category:Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Shiga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Shiga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Shiga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq\]] [[Category:qqq]][[en:Shiga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Shiga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:滋賀縣護國神社]][[sv:Shigaken-gokoku-jinja]][[simple:Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:Shiga Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:Shiga Gokoku Shrine]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \[[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category:qq 2]]\[[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category:qq 2]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Shigaken-gokoku-jinja02b3200.jpg|map_type=Japan|coordinates=|map_size=|map_alt=|map_relief=|map_caption=|location=|festival=|consecration_year=1869|type=''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'')|leadership=|website=|architecture_style=|founded_by=|established=|date_destroyed=}} {{ill|Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|ja|滋賀県護國神社|sv|Shigaken-gokoku-jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Hikone City, [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]] or a shrine for people who died in wars.{{Cite web|date=2017-09-05|title=日本彦根旅游指南|url=https://visit.hikoneshi.com/en/plan_your_visit/sightseeing/temples-shrines/shiga-gokoku-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-22|language=en-US}} There are more than 34,000 people who died in wars from the prefecture. These people are honored in the shrine. The wars are the [[Boshin War]], the [[Satsuma Rebellion]], the [[first Sino-Japanese War]], the [[Russo-Japanese War]], and [[World War II]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]] which means a highly ranked shrine. Gokoku shrines were made to honor people who died in wars. They are led by [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They used to be called [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] but the name was changed in 1939. == History == In the year 1869 a memorial was made in {{ill|Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)|lt=Ryutani Temple|ja|龍潭寺_(彦根市)}} for dead soldiers. 26 people died in the [[Boshin War]] from the city and they were honored in the temple. In [[1875]] (Meiji 8), the government said it should be made into a shrine. {{Ill|Ii Naonori|en}}, the lord of Hikone moved it to the current place. In [[1939]], it was renamed 'Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine' by [[Home Ministry|the Minister of Home Affairs]] . After [[World War II]] it was renamed to Sasanami Shrine. It was listed as a [[Beppyo shrine]]. In October [[1953]], after the occupation ended, the name was changed back. == Related pages == * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) == References == {{reflist}} == Other websites == * [http://www.shigagokoku.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Shiga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] {{Gokoku Shrines}} [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Coordinates on Wikidata]] [[Category:Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Shiga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Shiga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Shiga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq\]] [[Category:qqq]][[en:Shiga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Shiga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:滋賀縣護國神社]][[sv:Shigaken-gokoku-jinja]][[simple:Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:Shiga Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:Shiga Gokoku Shrine]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \[[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category:qq 2]]\[[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category:qq 2]] [[Category:New]]

It is a [[Beppyo shrine]] which means a highly ranked shrine. Gokoku shrines were made to honor people who died in wars. They are led by [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They used to be called [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] but the name was changed in 1939. == History == In the year 1869 a memorial was made in {{ill|Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)|lt=Ryutani Temple|ja|龍潭寺_(彦根市)}} for dead soldiers. 26 people died in the [[Boshin War]] from the city and they were honored in the temple. In [[1875]] (Meiji 8), the government said it should be made into a shrine. {{Ill|Ii Naonori|en}}, the lord of Hikone moved it to the current place. In [[1939]], it was renamed 'Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine' by [[Home Ministry|the Minister of Home Affairs]] . After [[World War II]] it was renamed to Sasanami Shrine. It was listed as a [[Beppyo shrine]]. (Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'')|leadership=|website=|architecture_style=|founded_by=|established=|date_destroyed=}} {{ill|Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|ja|滋賀県護國神社|sv|Shigaken-gokoku-jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Hikone City, [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]] or a shrine for people who died in wars.{{Cite web|date=2017-09-05|title=日本彦根旅游指南|url=https://visit.hikoneshi.com/en/plan_your_visit/sightseeing/temples-shrines/shiga-gokoku-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-22|language=en-US}} There are more than 34,000 people who died in wars from the prefecture. These people are honored in the shrine. The wars are the [[Boshin War]], the [[Satsuma Rebellion]], the [[first Sino-Japanese War]], the [[Russo-Japanese War]], and [[World War II]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]] which means a highly ranked shrine. Gokoku shrines were made to honor people who died in wars. They are led by [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They used to be called [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] but the name was changed in 1939. == History == In the year 1869 a memorial was made in {{ill|Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)|lt=Ryutani Temple|ja|龍潭寺_(彦根市)}} for dead soldiers. 26 people died in the [[Boshin War]] from the city and they were honored in the temple. In [[1875]] (Meiji 8), the government said it should be made into a shrine. {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Ii Naonori|en}}, the lord of Hikone moved it to the current place. In [[1939]], it was renamed 'Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine' by [[Home Ministry|the Minister of Home Affairs]] . After [[World War II]] it was renamed to Sasanami Shrine. It was listed as a [[Beppyo shrine]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]] or a shrine for people who died in wars.{{Cite web|date=2017-09-05|title=日本彦根旅游指南|url=https://visit.hikoneshi.com/en/plan_your_visit/sightseeing/temples-shrines/shiga-gokoku-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-22|language=en-US}} There are more than 34,000 people who died in wars from the prefecture. These people are honored in the shrine. The wars are the [[Boshin War]], the [[Satsuma Rebellion]], the [[first Sino-Japanese War]], the [[Russo-Japanese War]], and [[World War II]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]] which means a highly ranked shrine. Gokoku shrines were made to honor people who died in wars. They are led by [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They used to be called [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] but the name was changed in 1939. == History == In the year 1869 a memorial was made in {{ill|Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)|lt=Ryutani Temple|ja|龍潭寺_(彦根市)|12=draft}} for dead soldiers. 26 people died in the [[Boshin War]] from the city and they were honored in the temple. In [[1875]] (Meiji 8), the government said it should be made into a shrine. {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Ii Naonori|en}}, the lord of Hikone moved it to the current place. In [[1939]], it was renamed 'Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine' by [[Home Ministry|the Minister of Home Affairs]] . After [[World War II]] it was renamed to Sasanami Shrine. It was listed as a [[Beppyo shrine]]. In October [[1953]], after the occupation ended, the name was changed back. == Related pages == * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) == References == {{reflist}} == Other websites == * [http://www.shigagokoku.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{Gokoku Shrines}} [[simple:Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Coordinates on Wikidata]] [[Category:Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Shiga Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:11\]] [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category: 5]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Shiga Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:Shiga Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:滋賀縣護國神社]] [[sv:Shigaken-gokoku-jinja]]

It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]] or a shrine for people who died in wars.{{Cite web|date=2017-09-05|title=日本彦根旅游指南|url=https://visit.hikoneshi.com/en/plan_your_visit/sightseeing/temples-shrines/shiga-gokoku-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-22|language=en-US}} There are more than 34,000 people who died in wars from the prefecture. These people are honored in the shrine. The wars are the [[Boshin War]], the [[Satsuma Rebellion]], the [[first Sino-Japanese War]], the [[Russo-Japanese War]], and [[World War II]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]] which means a highly Ranked shrine. Gokoku shrines were made to honor people who died in wars. They are led by [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They used to be called [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] but the name was changed in 1939. == History == In the year 1869 a memorial was made in {{ill|Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)|lt=Ryutani Temple|ja|龍潭寺_(彦根市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} for dead soldiers. 26 people died in the [[Boshin War]] from the city and they were honored in the temple. In [[1875]] (Meiji 8), the government said it should be made into a shrine. {{ill|Ii Naonori|en}}, the lord of Hikone moved it to the current place. In [[1939]], it was renamed 'Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine' by [[Home Ministry|the Minister of Home Affairs]] . After [[World War II]] it was renamed to Sasanami Shrine. It was listed as a [[Beppyo shrine]]. In October [[1953]], after the occupation ended, the name was changed back. == Related pages == * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) == References == {{reflist}} == Other websites == * [http://www.shigagokoku.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{Gokoku Shrines}} [[simple:Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Coordinates on Wikidata]] [[Category:Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Shiga Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 8]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shiga Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Shiga Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:Shiga Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:滋賀縣護國神社]] [[sv:Shigaken-gokoku-jinja]]

revision.comment: 24 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:滋賀縣護國神社 up to rev 102003640
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:04 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称 = 滋賀縣護國神社 |画像 = [[画像:Shigaken-gokoku-jinja02b3200.jpg|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[滋賀県]][[彦根市]]尾末町1-59 |位置 = {{Coord|35|16|30|N|136|15|23|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 国事殉難者 |創建 = [[1876年]]（[[明治]]9年） |社格 = [[内務大臣 (日本)|内務大臣]]指定[[護国神社]]・[[別表神社]] |札所等 = [[井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり]]第7番 |例祭 = [[4月5日]]
[[10月5日]] |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=滋賀縣護國神社|position=bottom|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|16|30|N|136|15|23|E}}}} }} '''滋賀縣護國神社'''（しがけんごこくじんじゃ）は、[[滋賀県]][[彦根市]]にある[[神社]]（[[護国神社]]）。[[戊辰戦争]]から[[第二次世界大戦]]までの滋賀県関係の戦歿者3万4千余柱を祀る。現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。 == 歴史 == [[1869年]]（[[明治]]2年）、彦根の大洞[[龍潭寺 (彦根市)|龍潭寺]]に[[戊辰戦争]]で戦死した[[彦根藩]]士26人の霊を祀る招魂碑が建てられたのが始まりである。[[1875年]]（明治8年）、元彦根藩主[[井伊直憲]]の主唱により招魂碑を神社に改造する旨の政府の通達が出され、招魂碑を現在地に移し、翌[[1876年]]（明治9年）、同地に社殿を造営・鎮座した。 [[1939年]]（[[昭和]]14年）に[[内務大臣 (日本)|内務大臣]]指定護国神社として「滋賀県護國神社」に改称した。[[太平洋戦争]]後の占領期に「沙々那美神社」と改称し、[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]に加列されている。 日本が主権を回復した[[1953年]]（昭和28年）10月に元の社名に復した。 == 境内 == * 本殿 * 祝詞舎 * 拝殿 * 翼廊 * 滋賀県英霊顕彰館 * 社務所 * 父の像 * 母の像 Shigaken-gokoku-jinja03b3840.jpg|拝殿 == 前後の札所 == ; [[井伊家ゆかりのふく福めぐり]] : 6 [[埋木舎]]　-　'''7 滋賀縣護國神社'''　-　8 [[長寿院]]（大洞弁財天） == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Shigaken-Gokoku-jinja}} * [http://www.shigagokoku.jp/　滋賀縣護國神社] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しかけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:護国神社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:彦根市の歴史]] [[Category:彦根市の建築物]]

It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]] or a shrine for people who died in wars.{{Cite web|date=2017-09-05|title=日本彦根旅游指南|url=https://visit.hikoneshi.com/en/plan_your_visit/sightseeing/temples-shrines/shiga-gokoku-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-22|language=en-US}} There are more than 34,000 people who died in wars from the prefecture. These people are honored in the shrine. The wars are the [[Boshin War]], the [[Satsuma Rebellion]], the [[first Sino-Japanese War]], the [[Russo-Japanese War]], and [[World War II]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]] which means a highly Ranked shrine. Gokoku shrines were made to honor people who died in wars. They are led by [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They used to be called [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] but the name was changed in 1939. == History == In the year 1869 a memorial was made in {{ill|Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)|lt=Ryutani Temple|ja|龍潭寺_(彦根市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} for dead soldiers. 26 people died in the [[Boshin War]] from the city and they were honored in the temple. In [[1875]] (Meiji 8), the government said it should be made into a shrine. {{ill|Ii Naonori|en}}, the lord of Hikone moved it to the current place. In [[1939]], it was renamed 'Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine' by [[Home Ministry|the Minister of Home Affairs]] . After [[World War II]] it was renamed to Sasanami Shrine. It was listed as a [[Beppyo shrine]]. In October [[1953]], after the occupation ended, the name was changed back. == Related pages == * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) == References == {{reflist}} == Other websites == * [http://www.shigagokoku.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ \ }} {{translated page|ja|滋賀縣護國神社|version=102003640|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Coordinates on Wikidata]] [[Category:Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Shiga Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 8]] [[simple:Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Shiga Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:Shiga Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:滋賀縣護國神社]] [[sv:Shigaken-gokoku-jinja]]

It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]] or a shrine for people who died in wars.{{Cite web|date=2017-09-05|title=日本彦根旅游指南|url=https://visit.hikoneshi.com/en/plan_your_visit/sightseeing/temples-shrines/shiga-gokoku-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-22|language=en-US}} There are more than 34,000 people who died in wars from the prefecture. These people are honored in the shrine. The wars are the [[Boshin War]], the [[Satsuma Rebellion]], the [[first Sino-Japanese War]], the [[Russo-Japanese War]], and [[World War II]]. It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]] which means a highly Ranked shrine. Gokoku shrines were made to honor people who died in wars. They are led by [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They used to be called [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] but the name was changed in 1939. == History == In the year 1869 a memorial was made in {{ill|Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)|lt=Ryutani Temple|ja|龍潭寺_(彦根市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)|qq=}} for dead soldiers. 26 people died in the [[Boshin War]] from the city and they were honored in the temple. In [[1875]] (Meiji 8), the government said it should be made into a shrine. {{ill|Ii Naonori|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Naonori|qq=}}, the lord of Hikone moved it to the current place. In [[1939]], it was renamed 'Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine' by [[Home Ministry|the Minister of Home Affairs]] . After [[World War II]] it was renamed to Sasanami Shrine. It was listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]]. In October [[1953]], after the occupation ended, the name was changed back. == Related pages == * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) == References == {{reflist}} == Other websites == * [http://www.shigagokoku.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ \ }} {{translated page|ja|滋賀縣護國神社|version=102003640|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Coordinates on Wikidata]] [[Category:Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 8]] [[simple:Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Shiga Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:Shiga Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:滋賀縣護國神社]] [[sv:Shigaken-gokoku-jinja]] [[Category:彦根市の建築物]] [[Category:彦根市の歴史]]

It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]] or a shrine for people who died in wars.{{Cite web|date=2017-09-05|title=日本彦根旅游指南|url=https://visit.hikoneshi.com/en/plan_your_visit/sightseeing/temples-shrines/shiga-gokoku-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-22|language=en-US}} There are more than 34,000 people who died in wars from the prefecture. These people are honored in the shrine. The wars are the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]], the [[Satsuma Rebellion]], the [[:en:First Sino-Japanese War|First Sino-Japanese War]], the [[Russo-Japanese War]], and [[World War II]]. It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]] which means a highly Ranked shrine. Gokoku shrines were made to honor people who died in wars. They are led by [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They used to be called [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] but the name was changed in 1939. == History == In the year 1869 a memorial was made in {{ill|Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)|lt=Ryutani Temple|ja|龍潭寺_(彦根市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)|qq=}} for dead soldiers. 26 people died in the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]] from the city and they were honored in the temple. In [[:en:1875|1875]] (Meiji 8), the government said it should be made into a shrine. {{ill|Ii Naonori|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Naonori|qq=}}, the lord of Hikone moved it to the current place. In [[:en:1939|1939]], it was renamed 'Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine' by [[:en:Home Ministry|the Minister of Home Affairs]] . After [[World War II]] it was renamed to Sasanami Shrine. It was listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]]. In October [[:en:1953|1953]], after the occupation ended, the name was changed back. == Related pages == * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) == References == {{reflist}} == Other websites == * [http://www.shigagokoku.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ \ }} {{translated page|ja|滋賀縣護國神社|version=102003640|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Coordinates on Wikidata]] [[Category:Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 8]] [[simple:Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Shiga Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:滋賀縣護國神社]] [[sv:Shigaken-gokoku-jinja]] [[Category:Buildings in Hikone, Shiga]] [[Category:History of Hikone, Shiga]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Shigaken-Gokoku-jinja}}

(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'')|leadership=|website=|architecture_style=|founded_by=|established=|date_destroyed=|prefecture={{ill|Hikone|qid=Q386635}}}} {{ill|Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|ja|滋賀県護國神社|1=Shiga Gokoku Shrine|sv|Shigaken-gokoku-jinja|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga Gokoku Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 滋賀縣護國神社}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Hikone City, [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]] or a shrine for people who died in wars.{{Cite web|date=2017-09-05|title=日本彦根旅游指南|url=https://visit.hikoneshi.com/en/plan_your_visit/sightseeing/temples-shrines/shiga-gokoku-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-22|language=en-US}} There are more than 34,000 people who died in wars from the prefecture. These people are honored in the shrine. The wars are the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]], the [[:en:Satsuma Rebellion|Satsuma Rebellion]], the [[:en:First Sino-Japanese War|First Sino-Japanese War]], the [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]], and [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]]. It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]] which means a highly Ranked shrine. Gokoku shrines were made to honor people who died in wars. They are led by [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]]. They used to be called [[:en:Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] but the name was changed in 1939. == History == In the year 1869 a memorial was made in {{ill|Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)|lt=Ryutani Temple|ja|龍潭寺_(彦根市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)|qq=}} for dead soldiers. 26 people died in the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]] from the city and they were honored in the temple. In [[:en:1875|1875]] (Meiji 8), the government said it should be made into a shrine. {{ill|Ii Naonori|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Naonori|qq=}}, the lord of Hikone moved it to the current place. In [[:en:1939|1939]], it was renamed 'Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine' by [[:en:Home Ministry|the Minister of Home Affairs]] . After [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] it was renamed to Sasanami Shrine. It was listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]]. In October [[:en:1953|1953]], after the occupation ended, the name was changed back. == Related pages == * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) == References == {{reflist}} == Other websites == * [http://www.shigagokoku.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ \ }} {{translated page|ja|滋賀縣護國神社|version=102003640|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Coordinates on Wikidata]] [[Category:Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 8]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Shiga Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:滋賀縣護國神社]] [[sv:Shigaken-gokoku-jinja]] [[Category:Buildings in Hikone, Shiga]] [[Category:History of Hikone, Shiga]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Shigaken-Gokoku-jinja}}

It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]] which means a highly Ranked shrine. Gokoku shrines were made to honor people who died in wars. They are led by [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]]. They used to be called [[:en:Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] but the name was changed in 1939. == History == In the year 1869 a memorial was made in {{ill|Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)|lt=Ryutani Temple|ja|龍潭寺_(彦根市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)|qq=}} for dead soldiers. 26 people died in the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]] from the city and they were honored in the temple. In [[:en:1939|1939]], it was renamed 'Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine' by [[:en:Home Ministry|the Minister of Home Affairs]] . After [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] it was renamed to Sasanami Shrine. (Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'')|leadership=|website=|architecture_style=|founded_by=|established=|date_destroyed=|prefecture={{ill|Hikone|WD=Q386635}}}} {{ill|Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|ja|滋賀県護國神社|1=Shiga Gokoku Shrine|sv|Shigaken-gokoku-jinja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga Gokoku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 滋賀縣護國神社}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Hikone City, [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]] or a shrine for people who died in wars.{{Cite web|date=2017-09-05|title=日本彦根旅游指南|url=https://visit.hikoneshi.com/en/plan_your_visit/sightseeing/temples-shrines/shiga-gokoku-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-22|language=en-US}} There are more than 34,000 people who died in wars from the prefecture. These people are honored in the shrine. The wars are the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]], the [[:en:Satsuma Rebellion|Satsuma Rebellion]], the [[:en:First Sino-Japanese War|First Sino-Japanese War]], the [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]], and [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]]. It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]] which means a highly Ranked shrine. Gokoku shrines were made to honor people who died in wars. They are led by [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]]. They used to be called [[:en:Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] but the name was changed in 1939. == History == In the year 1869 a memorial was made in {{ill|Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)|lt=Ryutani Temple|ja|龍潭寺_(彦根市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)|qq=}} for dead soldiers. 26 people died in the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]] from the city and they were honored in the temple. In [[:en:1875|1875]] (Meiji 8), the government said it should be made into a shrine. {{ill|Ii Naonori|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Naonori|qq=}}, the lord of Hikone moved it to the current place. In [[:en:1939|1939]], it was renamed 'Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine' by [[:en:Home Ministry|the Minister of Home Affairs]] . After [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] it was renamed to Sasanami Shrine. It was listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]]. In October [[:en:1953|1953]], after the occupation ended, the name was changed back. == Related pages == * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) == References == {{reflist}} == Other websites == * [http://www.shigagokoku.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ \ }} {{translated page|ja|滋賀縣護國神社|version=102003640|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Coordinates on Wikidata]] [[Category:Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 8]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:滋賀縣護國神社]] [[sv:Shigaken-gokoku-jinja]] [[Category:Buildings in Hikone, Shiga]] [[Category:History of Hikone, Shiga]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Shigaken-Gokoku-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11564971}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Shigaken-Gokoku-jinja]][[ja:滋賀縣護國神社]][[simple:Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine]][[sv:Shigaken-gokoku-jinja]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11564971]] Wikidata name: Shigaken-Gokoku-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=File:Shigaken-gokoku-jinja02b3200.jpg|map_type=Japan|coordinates={{Coord|35.275|N|136.2563888888889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|map_size=|map_alt=|map_relief=|map_caption=|location=|festival=|consecration_year=1869|type=''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'')|leadership=|website=|architecture_style=|founded_by=|established=|date_destroyed=|prefecture={{ill|Hikone|WD=Q386635}}}} {{ill|Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|ja|滋賀県護國神社|1=Shiga Gokoku Shrine|sv|Shigaken-gokoku-jinja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga Gokoku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 滋賀縣護國神社}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Hikone City, [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]] or a shrine for people who died in wars.{{Cite web|date=2017-09-05|title=日本彦根旅游指南|url=https://visit.hikoneshi.com/en/plan_your_visit/sightseeing/temples-shrines/shiga-gokoku-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-22|language=en-US}} There are more than 34,000 people who died in wars from the prefecture. These people are honored in the shrine. The wars are the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]], the [[:en:Satsuma Rebellion|Satsuma Rebellion]], the [[:en:First Sino-Japanese War|First Sino-Japanese War]], the [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]], and [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]]. It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]] which means a highly Ranked shrine. Gokoku shrines were made to honor people who died in wars. They are led by [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]]. They used to be called [[:en:Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] but the name was changed in 1939. == History == In the year 1869 a memorial was made in {{ill|Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)|lt=Ryutani Temple|ja|龍潭寺_(彦根市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)|qq=}} for dead soldiers. 26 people died in the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]] from the city and they were honored in the temple. In [[:en:1875|1875]] (Meiji 8), the government said it should be made into a shrine. {{ill|Ii Naonori|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Naonori|qq=}}, the lord of Hikone moved it to the current place. In [[:en:1939|1939]], it was renamed 'Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine' by [[:en:Home Ministry|the Minister of Home Affairs]] . After [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] it was renamed to Sasanami Shrine. It was listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]]. In October [[:en:1953|1953]], after the occupation ended, the name was changed back. == Related pages == * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) == References == {{reflist}} == Other websites == * [http://www.shigagokoku.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ \ }} {{translated page|ja|滋賀縣護國神社|version=102003640|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Coordinates on Wikidata]] [[Category:Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 8]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:滋賀縣護國神社]] [[sv:Shigaken-gokoku-jinja]] [[Category:Buildings in Hikone, Shiga]] [[Category:History of Hikone, Shiga]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Shigaken-Gokoku-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11564971}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

It was listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]]. In October [[:en:1953|1953]], after the occupation ended, the name was changed back. == Related pages == * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) == References == {{reflist}} == Other websites == * [http://www.shigagokoku.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ \ }} {{translated page|ja|滋賀縣護國神社|version=102003640|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Coordinates on Wikidata]] [[Category:Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 8]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:滋賀縣護國神社]] [[sv:Shigaken-gokoku-jinja]] [[Category:Buildings in Hikone, Shiga]] [[Category:History of Hikone, Shiga]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Shigaken-Gokoku-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11564971}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Shigaken-Gokoku-jinja]][[ja:滋賀縣護國神社]][[simple:Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine]][[sv:Shigaken-gokoku-jinja]] [[commons:Category:Shigaken-Gokoku-jinja]][[ja:滋賀縣護國神社]][[simple:Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine]][[sv:Shigaken-gokoku-jinja]] [[sn:commons:Category:Shigaken-Gokoku-jinja]][[ja:滋賀縣護國神社]][[simple:Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine]][[sv:Shigaken-gokoku-jinja]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11564971]] Wikidata name: Shigaken-Gokoku-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=File:Shigaken-gokoku-jinja02b3200.jpg|map_type=Japan|coordinates={{Coord|35.275|N|136.2563888888889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|map_size=|map_alt=|map_relief=|map_caption=|location=|festival=|consecration_year=1869|type=''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'')|leadership=|website=|architecture_style=|founded_by=|established=|date_destroyed=|prefecture={{ill|Hikone|WD=Q386635}}}} {{ill|Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|ja|滋賀県護國神社|1=Shiga Gokoku Shrine|sv|Shigaken-gokoku-jinja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga Gokoku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 滋賀縣護國神社}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Hikone City, [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]] or a shrine for people who died in wars.{{Cite web|date=2017-09-05|title=日本彦根旅游指南|url=https://visit.hikoneshi.com/en/plan_your_visit/sightseeing/temples-shrines/shiga-gokoku-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-22|language=en-US}} There are more than 34,000 people who died in wars from the prefecture. These people are honored in the shrine. The wars are the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]], the [[:en:Satsuma Rebellion|Satsuma Rebellion]], the [[:en:First Sino-Japanese War|First Sino-Japanese War]], the [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]], and [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]]. It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]] which means a highly Ranked shrine. Gokoku shrines were made to honor people who died in wars. They are led by [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]]. They used to be called [[:en:Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] but the name was changed in 1939. == History == In the year 1869 a memorial was made in {{ill|Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)|lt=Ryutani Temple|ja|龍潭寺_(彦根市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)|qq=}} for dead soldiers. 26 people died in the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]] from the city and they were honored in the temple. In [[:en:1875|1875]] (Meiji 8), the government said it should be made into a shrine. {{ill|Ii Naonori|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Naonori|qq=}}, the lord of Hikone moved it to the current place. In [[:en:1939|1939]], it was renamed 'Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine' by [[:en:Home Ministry|the Minister of Home Affairs]] . After [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] it was renamed to Sasanami Shrine. It was listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]]. In October [[:en:1953|1953]], after the occupation ended, the name was changed back. == Related pages == * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) == References == {{reflist}} == Other websites == * [http://www.shigagokoku.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ \ }} {{translated page|ja|滋賀縣護國神社|version=102003640|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[simple:Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:滋賀縣護國神社]] [[sv:Shigaken-gokoku-jinja]] {{Commons category|Shigaken-Gokoku-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11564971}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Hikone, Shiga]] [[Category:Completion tier 8]] [[Category:Coordinates on Wikidata]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:History of Hikone, Shiga]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]] [[Category:Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

It was listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]]. In October [[:en:1953|1953]], after the occupation ended, the name was changed back. == Related pages == * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) == References == {{reflist}} == Other websites == * [http://www.shigagokoku.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ \ }} {{translated page|ja|滋賀縣護國神社|version=102003640|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[simple:Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:滋賀縣護國神社]] [[sv:Shigaken-gokoku-jinja]] {{Commons category|Shigaken-Gokoku-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11564971}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Hikone, Shiga]] [[Category:Completion tier 8]] [[Category:Coordinates on Wikidata]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:History of Hikone, Shiga]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]] [[Category:Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]] or a shrine for people who died in wars.{{Cite web|date=2017-09-05|title=日本彦根旅游指南|url=https://visit.hikoneshi.com/en/plan_your_visit/sightseeing/temples-shrines/shiga-gokoku-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-22|language=en-US}} There are more than 34,000 people who died in wars from the prefecture. These people are honored in the shrine. The wars are the {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War}}, the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|en|Satsuma Rebellion|WD=Q714820|lt=Satsuma Rebellion}}, the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|en|First Sino-Japanese War|WD=Q178687|lt=First Sino-Japanese War}}, the {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|en|Russo-Japanese War|WD=Q159950|lt=Russo-Japanese War}}, and {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War}}. It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]] which means a highly Ranked shrine. Gokoku shrines were made to honor people who died in wars. They are led by {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|en|Yasukuni Shrine|WD=Q242803|lt=Yasukuni Shrine}}. They used to be called {{ill|Shōkonsha|en|Shōkonsha|WD=Q11497810|lt=Shokonsha}} but the name was changed in 1939. == History == In the year 1869 a memorial was made in {{ill|Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)|lt=Ryutani Temple|ja|龍潭寺_(彦根市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)|qq=}} for dead soldiers. 26 people died in the {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War}} from the city and they were honored in the temple. In {{ill|1939|en|1939|WD=Q18639|lt=1939}}, it was renamed 'Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine' by {{ill|Home Ministry|en|Home Ministry|WD=Q1142265|lt=the Minister of Home Affairs}} . After {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War}} it was renamed to Sasanami Shrine. It was listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]]. In October {{ill|1953|en|1953|WD=Q18585|lt=1953}}, after the occupation ended, the name was changed back. == Related pages == * {{ill|Martyrs' shrines (China)|en|Martyrs' shrines (China)|WD=Q6800982|lt=Martyr's Shrines}} * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * {{ill|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|en|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|WD=Q1541043|lt=Tomb of the Unknown Soldier}} * {{ill|Arlington National Cemetery|en|Arlington National Cemetery|WD=Q216344|lt=Arlington National Cemetery}} * {{ill|Valhalla|en|Valhalla|WD=Q177860|lt=Valhalla}} (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) == References == {{reflist}} == Other websites == * [http://www.shigagokoku.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ \ }} {{translated page|ja|滋賀縣護國神社|version=102003640|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[simple:Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:滋賀縣護國神社]] [[sv:Shigaken-gokoku-jinja]] {{Commons category|Shigaken-Gokoku-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11564971}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Hikone, Shiga]] [[Category:Completion tier 8]] [[Category:Coordinates on Wikidata]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:History of Hikone, Shiga]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]] [[Category:Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

In {{ill|1875|en|1875|WD=Q7764|lt=1875}} (Meiji 8), the government said it should be made into a shrine. {{ill|Ii Naonori|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Naonori|qq=}}, the lord of Hikone moved it to the current place. In {{ill|1939|en|1939|WD=Q18639|lt=1939}}, it was renamed 'Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine' by {{ill|Home Ministry|en|Home Ministry|WD=Q1142265|lt=the Minister of Home Affairs}} . After {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War}} it was renamed to Sasanami Shrine. It was listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]]. In October {{ill|1953|en|1953|WD=Q18585|lt=1953}}, after the occupation ended, the name was changed back. == Related pages == * {{ill|Martyrs' shrines (China)|en|Martyrs' shrines (China)|WD=Q6800982|lt=Martyr's Shrines}} * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * {{ill|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|en|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|WD=Q1541043|lt=Tomb of the Unknown Soldier}} * {{ill|Arlington National Cemetery|en|Arlington National Cemetery|WD=Q216344|lt=Arlington National Cemetery}} * {{ill|Valhalla|en|Valhalla|WD=Q177860|lt=Valhalla}} (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) == References == {{reflist}} == Other websites == * [http://www.shigagokoku.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ \ }} {{translated page|ja|滋賀縣護國神社|version=102003640|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[simple:Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:滋賀縣護國神社]] [[sv:Shigaken-gokoku-jinja]] {{Commons category|Shigaken-Gokoku-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11564971}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Hikone, Shiga]] [[Category:Completion tier 8]] [[Category:Coordinates on Wikidata]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:History of Hikone, Shiga]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]] [[Category:Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

They are led by {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|en|Yasukuni Shrine|WD=Q242803|lt=Yasukuni Shrine}}. After {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War}} it was renamed to Sasanami Shrine. It was listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]]. In October {{ill|1953|en|1953|WD=Q18585|lt=1953}}, after the occupation ended, the name was changed back. == Related pages == * {{ill|Martyrs' shrines (China)|en|Martyrs' shrines (China)|WD=Q6800982|lt=Martyr's Shrines}} * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * {{ill|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|en|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|WD=Q1541043|lt=Tomb of the Unknown Soldier}} * {{ill|Arlington National Cemetery|en|Arlington National Cemetery|WD=Q216344|lt=Arlington National Cemetery}} * {{ill|Valhalla|en|Valhalla|WD=Q177860|lt=Valhalla}} (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) == References == {{reflist}} == Other websites == * [http://www.shigagokoku.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ \ }} {{translated page|ja|滋賀縣護國神社|version=102003640|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[simple:Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:滋賀縣護國神社]] [[sv:Shigaken-gokoku-jinja]] {{Commons category|Shigaken-Gokoku-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11564971}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Hikone, Shiga]] [[Category:Completion tier 8]] [[Category:Coordinates on Wikidata]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:History of Hikone, Shiga]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]] [[Category:Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

In October {{ill|1953|en|1953|WD=Q18585|lt=1953|1=1953}}, after the occupation ended, the name was changed back. == Related pages == * {{ill|Martyrs' shrines (China)|en|Martyrs' shrines (China)|WD=Q6800982|lt=Martyr's Shrines|1=Martyrs' shrines (China)}} * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * {{ill|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|en|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|WD=Q1541043|lt=Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|1=Tomb of the Unknown Soldier}} * {{ill|Arlington National Cemetery|en|Arlington National Cemetery|WD=Q216344|lt=Arlington National Cemetery|1=Arlington National Cemetery}} * {{ill|Valhalla|en|Valhalla|WD=Q177860|lt=Valhalla|1=Valhalla}} (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) == References == {{reflist}} == Other websites == * [http://www.shigagokoku.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ \ }} {{translated page|ja|滋賀縣護國神社|version=102003640|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[simple:Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:滋賀縣護國神社]] [[sv:Shigaken-gokoku-jinja]] {{Commons category|Shigaken-Gokoku-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11564971}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Hikone, Shiga]] [[Category:Completion tier 8]] [[Category:Coordinates on Wikidata]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:History of Hikone, Shiga]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]] [[Category:Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Shigaken-Gokoku-jinja]][[ja:滋賀縣護國神社]][[simple:Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine]][[sv:Shigaken-gokoku-jinja]] [[co:commons:Category:Shigaken-Gokoku-jinja]][[ja:滋賀縣護國神社]][[simple:Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine]][[sv:Shigaken-gokoku-jinja]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Shigaken-Gokoku-jinja]][[ja:滋賀縣護國神社]][[simple:Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine]][[sv:Shigaken-gokoku-jinja]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=File:Shigaken-gokoku-jinja02b3200.jpg|map_type=Japan|coordinates={{Coord|35.275|N|136.2563888888889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|map_size=|map_alt=|map_relief=|map_caption=|location=|festival=|consecration_year=1869|type=''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'')|leadership=|website=|architecture_style=|founded_by=|established=|date_destroyed=|prefecture={{ill|Hikone|WD=Q386635|1=Hikone, Shiga}}}} {{ill|Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|ja|滋賀県護國神社|1=Shiga Gokoku Shrine|sv|Shigaken-gokoku-jinja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga Gokoku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 滋賀縣護國神社}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Hikone City, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]] or a shrine for people who died in wars.{{Cite web|date=2017-09-05|title=日本彦根旅游指南|url=https://visit.hikoneshi.com/en/plan_your_visit/sightseeing/temples-shrines/shiga-gokoku-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-22|language=en-US}} There are more than 34,000 people who died in wars from the prefecture. These people are honored in the shrine. The wars are the {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War|1=Boshin War}}, the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|en|Satsuma Rebellion|WD=Q714820|lt=Satsuma Rebellion|1=Satsuma Rebellion}}, the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|en|First Sino-Japanese War|WD=Q178687|lt=First Sino-Japanese War|1=First Sino-Japanese War}}, the {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|en|Russo-Japanese War|WD=Q159950|lt=Russo-Japanese War|1=Russo-Japanese War}}, and {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}}. It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]] which means a highly Ranked shrine. Gokoku shrines were made to honor people who died in wars. They are led by {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|en|Yasukuni Shrine|WD=Q242803|lt=Yasukuni Shrine|1=Yasukuni Shrine}}. They used to be called {{ill|Shōkonsha|en|Shōkonsha|WD=Q11497810|lt=Shokonsha|1=Shōkonsha}} but the name was changed in 1939. == History == In the year 1869 a memorial was made in {{ill|Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)|lt=Ryutani Temple|ja|龍潭寺_(彦根市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)|qq=}} for dead soldiers. 26 people died in the {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War|1=Boshin War}} from the city and they were honored in the temple. In {{ill|1875|en|1875|WD=Q7764|lt=1875|1=1875}} (Meiji 8), the government said it should be made into a shrine. {{ill|Ii Naonori|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Naonori|qq=}}, the lord of Hikone moved it to the current place. In {{ill|1939|en|1939|WD=Q18639|lt=1939|1=1939}}, it was renamed 'Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine' by {{ill|Home Ministry|en|Home Ministry|WD=Q1142265|lt=the Minister of Home Affairs|1=Home Ministry}} . After {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}} it was renamed to Sasanami Shrine. It was listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]]. In October {{ill|1953|en|1953|WD=Q18585|lt=1953|1=1953}}, after the occupation ended, the name was changed back. == Related pages == * {{ill|Martyrs' shrines (China)|en|Martyrs' shrines (China)|WD=Q6800982|lt=Martyr's Shrines|1=Martyrs' shrines (China)}} * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * {{ill|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|en|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|WD=Q1541043|lt=Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|1=Tomb of the Unknown Soldier}} * {{ill|Arlington National Cemetery|en|Arlington National Cemetery|WD=Q216344|lt=Arlington National Cemetery|1=Arlington National Cemetery}} * {{ill|Valhalla|en|Valhalla|WD=Q177860|lt=Valhalla|1=Valhalla}} (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) == References == {{reflist}} == Other websites == * [http://www.shigagokoku.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ \ }} {{translated page|ja|滋賀縣護國神社|version=102003640|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[simple:Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:滋賀縣護國神社]] [[sv:Shigaken-gokoku-jinja]] {{Commons category|Shigaken-Gokoku-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11564971}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Hikone, Shiga]] [[Category:Completion tier 8]] [[Category:Coordinates on Wikidata]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:History of Hikone, Shiga]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]] [[Category:Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

After {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}} it was renamed to Sasanami Shrine. It was listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]]. In October {{ill|1953|en|1953|WD=Q18585|lt=1953|1=1953}}, after the occupation ended, the name was changed back. == Related pages == * {{ill|Martyrs' shrines (China)|en|Martyrs' shrines (China)|WD=Q6800982|lt=Martyr's Shrines|1=Martyrs' shrines (China)}} * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * {{ill|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|en|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|WD=Q1541043|lt=Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|1=Tomb of the Unknown Soldier}} * {{ill|Arlington National Cemetery|en|Arlington National Cemetery|WD=Q216344|lt=Arlington National Cemetery|1=Arlington National Cemetery}} * {{ill|Valhalla|en|Valhalla|WD=Q177860|lt=Valhalla|1=Valhalla}} (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) == References == {{reflist}} == Other websites == * [http://www.shigagokoku.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ \ }} {{translated page|ja|滋賀縣護國神社|version=102003640|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[simple:Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:滋賀縣護國神社]] [[sv:Shigaken-gokoku-jinja]] {{Commons category|Shigaken-Gokoku-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11564971}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Hikone, Shiga]] [[Category:Coordinates on Wikidata]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:History of Hikone, Shiga]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]] [[Category:Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
