[[co:commons:Category:Shigaken-Gokoku-jinja]][[ja:滋賀縣護國神社]][[simple:Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine]][[sv:Shigaken-gokoku-jinja]] [[co:commons:Category:Shigaken-Gokoku-jinja]][[ja:滋賀縣護國神社]][[simple:Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine]][[sv:Shigaken-gokoku-jinja]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Shigaken-Gokoku-jinja]][[ja:滋賀縣護國神社]][[simple:Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine]][[sv:Shigaken-gokoku-jinja]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=File:Shigaken-gokoku-jinja02b3200.jpg|map_type=Japan|coordinates={{Coord|35.275|N|136.2563888888889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|map_size=|map_alt=|map_relief=|map_caption=|location=|festival=|consecration_year=1869|type=''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'')|leadership=|website=|architecture_style=|founded_by=|established=|date_destroyed=|prefecture={{ill|Hikone|WD=Q386635|1=Hikone, Shiga}}}} {{ill|Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|ja|滋賀県護國神社|1=Shiga Gokoku Shrine|sv|Shigaken-gokoku-jinja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga Gokoku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 滋賀縣護國神社}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Hikone City, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]] or a shrine for people who died in wars.{{Cite web|date=2017-09-05|title=日本彦根旅游指南|url=https://visit.hikoneshi.com/en/plan_your_visit/sightseeing/temples-shrines/shiga-gokoku-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-22|language=en-US}} There are more than 34,000 people who died in wars from the prefecture. These people are honored in the shrine. The wars are the {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War|1=Boshin War}}, the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|en|Satsuma Rebellion|WD=Q714820|lt=Satsuma Rebellion|1=Satsuma Rebellion}}, the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|en|First Sino-Japanese War|WD=Q178687|lt=First Sino-Japanese War|1=First Sino-Japanese War}}, the {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|en|Russo-Japanese War|WD=Q159950|lt=Russo-Japanese War|1=Russo-Japanese War}}, and {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}}. It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]] which means a highly Ranked shrine. Gokoku shrines were made to honor people who died in wars. They are led by {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|en|Yasukuni Shrine|WD=Q242803|lt=Yasukuni Shrine|1=Yasukuni Shrine}}. They used to be called {{ill|Shōkonsha|en|Shōkonsha|WD=Q11497810|lt=Shokonsha|1=Shōkonsha}} but the name was changed in 1939. == History == In the year 1869 a memorial was made in {{ill|Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)|lt=Ryutani Temple|ja|龍潭寺_(彦根市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryutanji Temple (Hikone City)|qq=}} for dead soldiers. 26 people died in the {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War|1=Boshin War}} from the city and they were honored in the temple. In {{ill|1875|en|1875|WD=Q7764|lt=1875|1=1875}} (Meiji 8), the government said it should be made into a shrine. {{ill|Ii Naonori|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii Naonori|qq=}}, the lord of Hikone moved it to the current place. In {{ill|1939|en|1939|WD=Q18639|lt=1939|1=1939}}, it was renamed 'Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine' by {{ill|Home Ministry|en|Home Ministry|WD=Q1142265|lt=the Minister of Home Affairs|1=Home Ministry}} . After {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}} it was renamed to Sasanami Shrine. It was listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]]. In October {{ill|1953|en|1953|WD=Q18585|lt=1953|1=1953}}, after the occupation ended, the name was changed back. == Related pages == * {{ill|Martyrs' shrines (China)|en|Martyrs' shrines (China)|WD=Q6800982|lt=Martyr's Shrines|1=Martyrs' shrines (China)}} * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * {{ill|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|en|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|WD=Q1541043|lt=Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|1=Tomb of the Unknown Soldier}} * {{ill|Arlington National Cemetery|en|Arlington National Cemetery|WD=Q216344|lt=Arlington National Cemetery|1=Arlington National Cemetery}} * {{ill|Valhalla|en|Valhalla|WD=Q177860|lt=Valhalla|1=Valhalla}} (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) == References == {{reflist}} == Other websites == * [http://www.shigagokoku.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ \ }} {{translated page|ja|滋賀縣護國神社|version=102003640|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[simple:Shiga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:滋賀縣護國神社]] [[sv:Shigaken-gokoku-jinja]] {{Commons category|Shigaken-Gokoku-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11564971}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hikone, Shiga]] [[Category:Coordinates on Wikidata]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:History of Hikone, Shiga]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]] [[Category:Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
